2606.27863.title: GNBAN: Graph Neural Basis Attention Networks for Long-Horizon Forecasting over Large Entity Sets
2606.27863.paper_summary: Demand forecasting at the bottom of a retail hierarchy requires predicting tens of thousands of correlated long-horizon series across products, stores, and regions. Modern systems must scale across massive catalogs, capture shared demand dynamics, and remain interpretable enough to be trusted. Classical statistical methods need a separate model per series and are hard to manage at scale; deep autoregressive models struggle as the joint state grows to tens of thousands of dimensions; and recent graph-based forecasters, while capturing cross-entity dependencies, often produce opaque long-horizon forecasts. We propose GNBAN (Graph Neural Basis Attention Network), an end-to-end architecture combining heterogeneous graph representation learning with an interpretable basis-decomposition head. Retail data are represented directly as a heterogeneous graph derived from the relational schema, so a single model serves the entire catalog. Rather than predicting the horizon directly, GNBAN decomposes each forecast into trend, seasonal, and generic components. Its key innovation is a per-basis attention mechanism: each basis function keeps its own learnable query and retrieves information independently from the entity's historical neighborhood, letting different bases specialize to distinct temporal patterns while preserving interpretability. On two large-scale benchmarks, M5 Walmart and Favorita Grocery Sales, evaluated under matched protocols, GNBAN improves volume-weighted WRMSSE by roughly 4-5% over a matched graph baseline. Qualitative analysis shows the learned decomposition exposes trend, seasonal, and residual demand drivers without post-hoc explanation methods. These results demonstrate that scalable relational forecasting and interpretable forecast decomposition can be achieved together in a unified graph-based framework.
2606.27863.paper_summary_zh: 需求預測在零售層級的底部需要預測數萬條相關的長期序列，涵蓋產品、商店和地區。現代系統必須能夠擴展到龐大的目錄，捕捉共享的需求動態，並保持足夠的可解釋性以獲得信任。傳統的統計方法需要為每個序列建立單獨的模型，並且在大規模管理上困難重重；深度自回歸模型在聯合狀態增長到數萬維時表現不佳；而最近的基於圖形的預測模型雖然能捕捉跨實體的依賴關係，但通常產生不透明的長期預測。我們提出了GNBAN（圖神經基礎注意力網絡），這是一種端到端架構，結合了異質圖表示學習和可解釋的基礎分解頭。零售數據直接表示為從關係模式衍生的異質圖，因此單一模型可以服務整個目錄。GNBAN並不是直接預測預測期，而是將每個預測分解為趨勢、季節性和通用組件。其關鍵創新是每個基礎的注意力機制：每個基礎函數保持自己的可學習查詢，並獨立從實體的歷史鄰域檢索信息，讓不同的基礎專注於不同的時間模式，同時保持可解釋性。在兩個大型基準測試中，M5 Walmart和Favorita Grocery Sales，在匹配的協議下進行評估，GNBAN相較於匹配的圖形基線，將體積加權的WRMSSE提高了約4-5%。質性分析顯示，學習到的分解揭示了趨勢、季節性和殘差需求驅動因素，而無需事後解釋方法。這些結果表明，可擴展的關係預測和可解釋的預測分解可以在統一的基於圖形的框架中同時實現。
2606.27863.author: Janak M. Patel et.al.
2606.27863.authors: Janak M. Patel, Anirudh Deodhar, Dagnachew Birru
